notes: Every frozen row was reconciled as an artifact: twenty rows are raw or cached vendor-AI descriptions promoted into the name field, and POPULAR plus OFTEN LIKED are ordering-interface badges.
The owner-domain ordering payload contains 36 lunch and 206 all-day presentations across 21 menu groups, all keyed by 242 unique menuItemId values. All 242 actual named presentations were restored.

Lunch, main, dinner-combo, and party-tray presentations remain distinct where the ordering source sells them separately; duplicate display names in different sections receive category-qualified internal IDs.
The Beverages and Soda groups are moved after every food group while all other source category and product order is preserved.

Asia Garden publishes no official allergen guide, complete ingredient disclosure, or cross-contact statement in the reviewed current sources, so all 242 real products remain allergenSourceType unavailable with empty fixed and mayContain arrays.

The seven frozen official-ingredients rows were all non-product description artifacts. Their shellfish, sesame, milk, wheat, or gluten claims are not applicable product-level verdicts and are removed with the artifacts.
Menu item names and the 46 raw ordering descriptions remain available to labeled Ingredient Intelligence, but restaurant-linked vendor text is not promoted to official fixed-allergen evidence.
The 154 cachedAIMenuItemDesc values are explicitly generated vendor content and are excluded from published descriptions, fixed allergens, cross-contact, and Ingredient Intelligence input.
The frozen generic HTML-card path selected description text and recommendation badges as product names, discarded 73 rows, and reduced the current 242-presentation menu to 22 invalid rows.

The current Next.js server payload contains a rawMenus object with the real menuItemName records and a separately labeled cachedAIMenuItemDesc map; the corrected parser consumes only raw menu records and ignores the generated description map.

The hash-pinned builder fails closed on source hashes, owner identity, disagreement between lunch and all-day raw payloads, exact menu/group counts, source ID uniqueness, modifier-template integrity, canonical ID uniqueness, and raw-versus-AI description counts.

Three focused catalog, reconciliation, and repaired-output tests pass. Two consecutive target-only repair runs preserved both the repaired target hash and the full non-target restaurant projection hash.
Per-restaurant application uses RESTAURANT_REPAIR_ONLY_ID so unrelated repair blocks cannot mutate other restaurants.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Captured the owner-domain homepage identifying the restaurant, Alexandria address, phone, and current same-domain ordering-menu link.
Captured the current lunch ordering surface and extracted its embedded raw menu payload containing 36 Lunch Special presentations and the same complete two-menu boundary as the all-day surface.
Captured the all-day ordering surface and extracted 206 all-day plus 36 lunch raw product records, 14 modifier templates, 46 raw descriptions, and a separate 154-entry cached AI-description map.
notes: A browser-like user agent was required because bare HTTP requests returned 403.
The lunch and all-day pages expose byte-different render states but identical rawMenus and cachedAIMenuItemDesc payloads.

sourceAttempts.outcome: The legacy configured route returned an internal error and was not used as the current source boundary.
notes: The owner homepage now links the location-specific order-online route.

findings.summary: All 22 frozen rows are non-products: twenty description strings and two recommendation badges were promoted into product names, while every real named menu presentation was absent.
findings.resolution: Removed all 22 artifacts and rebuilt the menu from 242 unique raw menuItemName records across the lunch and all-day surfaces.

findings.summary: The ordering payload explicitly labels 154 descriptions as cached AI content, but the frozen extractor treated generated descriptions as menu products and ingredient evidence.
findings.resolution: Separated rawMenus from cachedAIMenuItemDesc, excluded every cached AI value from published descriptions and allergen inference, and made the parser fail closed if the reviewed count changes.

findings.summary: Seven frozen description artifacts were labeled official-ingredients and assigned fixed allergens even though no real product row or official allergen source supported that classification.
findings.resolution: Removed the seven invalid artifact claims, kept every current product unavailable for official allergens, and retained only labeled Ingredient Intelligence based on raw menu titles and the 46 non-AI ordering descriptions.

findings.summary: The frozen 22-row output omitted the entire 242-presentation current menu, including lunch, appetizers, soups, noodles, entrees, combos, specialties, sides, party trays, and beverages.
findings.resolution: Restored all 242 source presentations, preserved separately sold variants, and put both beverage sections at the end.

repairs.summary: Replaced 22 description/badge artifacts with all 242 current raw menu presentations, excluded 154 cached AI descriptions, removed seven spurious official allergen artifact claims, and constrained application to Asia Garden.
